mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a signal-weighted portfolio Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with a tight labor market create restrictive financial conditions that suppress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 60% of triggered instances despite the restrictive financial conditions signal, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDP growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust employment expansion and wage income generation, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with a mean forward return below 0.0% and a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation alongside stagnant industrial production signals stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal yields positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered cases, or predictive power vanishes when inflation exceeds 4.0% in out-of-sample tests.
